business_hours.seasonal_hours: Saturday crew from 8 AM to 12 PM for scheduled inspections only; no new intake on Saturdays.

contact_info.name: Carlos
contact_info.context: Primary contact for 24/7 emergency line, first tier of escalation.

contact_info.name: Luis
contact_info.context: Current on-call technician, second tier of emergency escalation if Carlos doesn't answer.

contact_info.name: Carlos
contact_info.context: First tier contact for emergency routing.

contact_info.name: Luis
contact_info.context: Second tier contact for emergency routing. This number rotates weekly and needs to be updated.

services_supported: Free estimates for commercial fire protection services
services_excluded: Calls from outside Texas and Louisiana
emergency_definition: Fire sprinkler head breaks or discharges
Fire alarm system malfunctions causing false alarms that won't stop
Complete fire alarm panel failures leaving a building unprotected
Any situation where a fire suppression system has activated
Monitoring company calls (e.g., ADT, SimpliSafe, any third-party)

order: If no answer, transfer to On-call Technician
If no answer, transfer to Dave Martinez (Operations Manager)
emergency_routing_rules.fallback_action: If no one picks up after all three attempts, collect caller's name, number, property address, and exact nature of the alarm or leak. Inform caller that someone WILL call back within 10 minutes. Send a simultaneous text notification to Dave, Carlos, and the on-call technician.

non_emergency_routing_rules.action: Clara is to take a message for all non-emergency inquiries.
non_emergency_routing_rules.notes: Clara should never promise a response time for non-emergencies. For Saturday new intake, take a message and handle it Monday.

call_transfer_rules.during_hours_transfer: Monitoring company calls (e.g., ADT, SimpliSafe, any third-party) should be treated as priority calls and transferred immediately to an appropriate internal contact (likely Carlos or the on-call technician).
notes: Monitoring company calls are considered emergencies and require immediate transfer.

integration_constraints: Clara should never create sprinkler jobs in ServiceTrade. This process must be done manually by the Patriot Fire Protection team due to specific job type classification requirements.
after_hours_flow_summary: During after-hours, Clara will first determine if the call is an emergency. If it is an emergency, Clara will attempt to transfer the call sequentially to Carlos (45 seconds timeout), then the on-call technician (45 seconds timeout), and finally Dave Martinez (45 seconds timeout). If no one answers, Clara will collect the caller's name, number, property address, and the exact nature of the alarm or leak, assuring them someone will call back within 10 minutes, and then send a simultaneous text notification to Dave, Carlos, and the on-call technician. If the call is a non-emergency, Clara will collect the caller's name, company name (if applicable), phone number, property address, and the type of service needed, informing them that they will receive a callback the next business day.
office_hours_flow_summary: During office hours (Monday-Friday, 8 AM-6 PM Central Time), Clara will handle calls. For emergencies, the routing follows the same escalation chain as after-hours emergencies. For non-emergencies, Clara will collect necessary information (name, company, phone, address, service type) and inform the caller they will be called back, mentioning free estimates for commercial services. On Saturdays (8 AM-12 PM), Clara will only process calls for scheduled inspections. Any new intake requests on Saturday will be handled as non-emergencies, with Clara taking a message and informing the caller it will be addressed on Monday. Monitoring company calls are considered priority and will be immediately transferred to an appropriate internal contact.
special_rules: Patriot Fire Protection is licensed only in Texas and Louisiana; calls from outside these states should be politely declined.
Clara can mention that free estimates are offered for commercial fire protection services.
Clara must never promise a response time for non-emergencies.
For emergencies, Clara can state that the aim is for a 30-minute response.
Monitoring company calls (e.g., ADT, SimpliSafe, any third-party) are priority calls and must be transferred immediately, even during business hours.
Saturday hours (8 AM - 12 PM) are strictly for scheduled inspections; new intake requests received on Saturday should be messaged and handled on Monday.
notes: Updated with onboarding data. Operational details confirmed.
